channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Father and Uncle of Slain New York Girl Given Life Sentences in Pakistan
item.description: The killing of the victim, a 14-year-old girl from Yonkers who was known for posting videos on TikTok, was premeditated, a court ruled.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman

media:content.media:credit: Saadullah Akhter for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The path outside this family home in Quetta, Pakistan, was where Hira Anwar, 14, was killed.

item.title: Tengo 53 años. Dejen de decirme que soy invisible
item.description: Nunca ha habido un mejor momento para ser una mujer de mediana edad.
item.dc:creator: Mireille Silcoff

item.title: Mamdani Burns Allies in Making a Big Bet for Congress and the Left
item.description: The strength of the mayor’s political brand will be tested on Tuesday, when his slate of leftist congressional candidates takes aim at Democratic incumbents.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed Claire Valdez, left, Brad Lander and Darializa Avila Chevalier in primary races on Tuesday.

item.title: A Reporter’s Memories Cross Two-Lane Highways in Missouri
item.description: “Kenrex” reimagines a notorious killing in Skidmore, Mo. Jesse McKinley wrote about the play — and the crime — after getting a tip from his son.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley

media:content.media:credit: Chase Castor for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Decades after a small-town killing made Skidmore, Mo., a point on the true-crime map, residents are protective of its legacy.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 21, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: For Half a Century, Capturing New York’s Human Moments
item.description: Lovers’ quarrels on sidewalks, acts of kindness on public transportation, friendships forged under awnings in the rain, and so much more of the city’s daily poetry.
item.dc:creator: Alex Vadukul

media:content.media:description: Metropolitan Diary, a column featuring readers’ stories, began appearing in November, 1976.

item.title: How Remote Work Has Helped a Generation of Working Parents
item.description: Post-pandemic, a new openness to accommodating family needs has made it possible for more mothers and fathers to balance work and parenting — particularly mothers of young children.
item.dc:creator: Claire Cain Miller

media:content.media:credit: Brad Trone for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Elizabeth Terhune, with her children in Santa Fe, N.M., worked from home when her youngest was born during the pandemic. Remote work has enabled more mothers of young children to stay in the labor force.

item.title: ‘What Do I Do? Do I Tell Her? How Do I Fix This?’
item.description: Some notable friends of Metropolitan Diary share their New York stories in the first of a special, limited series of columns marking the feature’s 50th anniversary.

item.title: ‘Dios es caboverdiano’: una pequeña nación en la cima tras un debut sensacional en el Mundial
item.description: Después de su empate con España, una mezcla de confianza y nerviosismo se ha apoderado del pequeño país africano, que espera repetir la hazaña frente a Uruguay.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean, Andre Amaral and Saikou Jammeh

media:content.media:description: El portero veterano de Cabo Verde, Josimar José Évora Dias, tras el empate a cero de su equipo contra España el lunes.

item.title: Wildfire in Central Utah Prompts Evacuation Order for Hundreds of People
item.description: The Iron fire had burned more than 13,000 acres by Saturday night. Containment efforts have been made more difficult by the region’s steep terrain.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao

media:content.media:credit: The Deseret News, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Residents evacuating as winds drive the Iron Fire toward Eureka, Utah, on Saturday.

item.title: Elon Musk Is Too Rich. We’ve Known That Since Plato.
item.description: The philosopher made a radical proposal about wealth.
item.dc:creator: David Lay Williams

item.title: Mideast Live Updates: New Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Set to Start in Switzerland
item.description: Vice President JD Vance was expected to meet with Iranian negotiators. But the conflict in Lebanon threatens efforts to reach a broader peace and keep the Strait of Hormuz open.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Abbas Araghchi, center, Iran’s foreign minister, and Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, left, the speaker of the Iranian parliament, arriving near Lucerne, Switzerland, on Sunday.

item.title: Luxury Kushner Project Collides With Albanian Discontent
item.description: Protests in Albania against plans for a luxury tourist site have become a cause célèbre for opponents of President Trump and his family. But the politics are local.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins

media:content.media:description: Protests in Tirana, Albania’s capital, against the government this month. Some Albanians worried that their cause was being hijacked by foreign activists.

item.title: Albanians Protest Corruption and Kushner-Linked Resort
item.description: For more than three weeks, thousands of Albanians have been protesting a planned luxury hotel development on the Adriatic coast that is partially financed by President Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

item.title: The Women Running Businesses Under Taliban Rules
item.description: With secondary education and most jobs out of reach, thousands of Afghan women have turned to entrepreneurship as the only path to make money and maintain a social life.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Kiana Hayeri

media:content.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times

item.title: The Dad Movie Canon: Films That Define Dad Cinema
item.description: What is it about a certain kind of film that appeals to fathers? Our writer took a stab at defining Dad Cinema and rounding up its greatest hits.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey
item.category: Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World (Movie)

media:content.media:credit: Clockwise from top left: Michael Weinstein/Castle Rock Entertainment; The Mirisch Company; Orion Pictures; Stephen Vaughan/Warner Bros.; Paramount Pictures/Moviepix, via Getty Images; Stephen Vaughan/20th Century Fox; Warner Bros.

item.title: Quote of the Day: No Shipwrecks Available to Stem Population Decline
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, June 21, 2026.

item.title: Drone Footage Captures Massive Fire at Dominican Resort
item.description: A large fire at a beach resort in the Dominican Republic forced the evacuation of nearly 1,700 guests and killed one person, emergency officials said.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:description: A screen shot showing the Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach Hotel on fire in Bayahibe, Dominican Republic, on Friday.

item.title: Trump, Claiming Vandalism, Says Reflecting Pool Will Likely Need to Be Drained
item.description: The pool has taken on clouds of algae after a hasty renovation. A three-time Olympian was charged with destroying government property after he says he touched one of the strands of blue paint peeling off the pool’s bottom.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim

media:content.media:description: Workers attempt to clean algae bloom from the newly renovated reflecting pool in Washington on Thursday.

item.title: Claude Guillemot, Ubisoft Co-Founder, Is Dead After Plane Crash in France
item.description: Claude Guillemot, 69, founded the video game company, which was also known for the hit Far Cry, with his brothers in 1986. He was killed in western France.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

item.title: What We Know About the Los Angeles Warehouse Fire
item.description: The fire began in a cold-storage facility in the Boyle Heights neighborhood and was still burning on Saturday, prompting a local emergency declaration.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: Crews work to extinguish a warehouse fire in the Boyle Heights section of Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: U.S. and Iranian Officials to Meet for Peace Talks in Switzerland
item.description: The next phase of talks to end the war in Iran is expected to begin on Sunday amid fighting in Lebanon and renewed confusion over the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A plane carrying an Iranian negotiating team landed at Zurich International Airport in Switzerland on Saturday.

item.title: A 1973 Knicks Championship Banner and a Childhood Friendship Endure
item.description: Two retired New York City Sanitation Department workers, friends since grade school, dusted off a celebratory banner they carried in 1973, the last time the Knicks won a championship.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman

item.title: Big Draw
item.description: Hannah Slovut-Einertson’s first Sunday puzzle for The Times presents a vertical challenge.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: A baby elephant on the way to help you with 89-Across.

item.title: Mona Khalil, Defender of Sea Turtles, Killed in an Israeli Strike in Lebanon
item.description: For a quarter century, Ms. Khalil ran a guesthouse and worked to protect endangered sea turtles who every summer lay their eggs on a stretch of beach near Tyre, Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai
item.category: Society for the Protection of Nature in Lebanon

media:content.media:description: Mona Khalil in Mansuri, Lebanon, in 2004.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Offer Contradictory Statements on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran’s military announced that it had closed the Strait of Hormuz around the same time on Saturday that Vice President JD Vance told Fox News the Trump administration had accomplished its goal of opening the strait.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: Man Is Arrested in Scotland After Rampage Leaves 5 Hurt
item.description: An advocacy group identified some of the victims as Muslims. Counterterrorism authorities are leading the investigation, but a motive for the attacks is so far unknown.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Robert Ormerod for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The police in Edinburgh received a report of an attack around 8:50 p.m. on Friday in the Sighthill area. Over the next 40 minutes, officers got reports of other attacks, which they believe are linked, in other parts of the city.

item.title: Huge Blaze Ravages Dominican Republic Beach Resort
item.description: The fire on Friday forced the emergency evacuation of nearly 1,700 guests. Authorities said at least one person, an Italian tourist, was killed.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:description: A screen shot showing the Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach Hotel on fire in Bayahibe, Dominican Republic, on Friday.

item.title: Former Gotti Associate Turned Councilman Arrested in Loan Scheme
item.description: John Alite, a borough council member in Englishtown, N.J., since 2025, had in recent years established himself as a man who learned from his mistakes.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:description: John Alite often posted online about leaving the mob life behind.

item.title: Remains of 3 People Are Found in Foreclosed House After Sale
item.description: The house in Connecticut had not been inspected before a winning bid was made at an auction, according to court records. The buyer discovered the bodies after entering the premises.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner

media:content.media:description: The new owner of a foreclosed house in Burlington, Conn., discovered the remains of three people in the property.

item.title: Wife of Spanish Prime Minister to Stand Trial for Corruption
item.description: Begoña Gómez faces charges of embezzlement, corruption, misappropriation of funds and influence peddling for helping favored companies win public contracts.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and José Bautista

media:content.media:description: Begoña Gomez and her husband Pedro Sanchez, the Prime Minister of Spain, at an event in Barcelona this month.

item.title: El estanque reflectante del monumento a Lincoln parece rechazar su cambio de imagen
item.description: El proyecto para renovar el estanque reflectante se terminó hace casi dos semanas. Desde entonces, no ha sido más que un dolor de cabeza para el gobierno de Trump.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi

media:content.media:description: Un equipo del Servicio de Parques Nacionales limpiando las algas del estanque reflectante el viernes.

item.title: The Newsroom in D.C. Tries to Keep the Newsstand as a Shopping Destination
item.description: The Newsroom, overflowing with magazines and newspapers on mismatched shelves, has endured as a symbol of a different Washington.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella and Alex Kent

media:content.media:description: Stephen Bota, owner of the Newsroom, one of the last newsstands in Washington.

item.title: New Strait of Hormuz Closure Announcement Threatens the Slow Uptick in Traffic
item.description: Fifty-five ships had passed through the strait on Saturday, the U.S. military said. But then Iran’s military said it was closing the waterway once again.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Jonathan Wolfe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: François Englert, Nobelist Who Helped Predict the ‘God Particle,’ Dies at 93
item.description: His work paved the way for the discovery of the Higgs boson, which explained how particles acquire mass, solving one of the deepest mysteries in physics.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Loeb McClain

media:content.media:description: François Englert in 2012 at his home in Brussels.

item.title: ‘Agonizing for Everyone’: After IVF Mix-up, Birthing Couple Keeps Baby
item.description: One Florida couple discovered the baby they birthed did not come from their embryo, but they will retain custody in an agreement with the biological couple.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz

item.title: 3 Hikers Die at Grand Canyon Amid ‘Dangerous’ High Temperatures
item.description: The hikers died at Grand Canyon National Park in separate episodes as temperatures peaked above 100 degrees, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

media:content.media:credit: John Burcham for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The South Kaibab Trail in Grand Canyon National Park, where one of three hikers died recently amid temperatures above 100 degrees.

item.title: How to Get Your Eyeliner Just Right
item.description: Tips on achieving a smudged, tight-lined or winged effect.
item.dc:creator: Chanel Parks

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Are Still Fighting, Several Cease-Fires Later
item.description: For the second time in two days, Lebanon was again at the center of whether the fragile U.S.-Iran deal would hold.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Rescue workers at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Qannarit village, southern Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: Obama and Mamdani Show How It’s Done
item.description: Shadow commemorations in Chicago and New York.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie

media:content.media:credit: Photographs by Jamie Lee Taete and José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times

item.title: Bowen Yang’s Mom’s Mapo Tofu
item.description: Spicy, tingly, savory perfection.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Linda Xiao for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.
media:content.media:description: Bowen Yang’s mapo tofu, adapted by The New York Times.

item.title: No End in Sight for U.S. Military Mission Along Border With Mexico
item.description: The patrols have helped push cartels and smugglers into more remote areas. But analysts have voiced concerns that the border missions will distract from training, drain resources and undermine readiness.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A soldier assigned to the southern border near Santa Teresa, N.M., last year.

item.title: South Korean Author Cho Nam-Joo on Self-Cultivation and Collective Change
item.description: I thought that my writing could be a small catalyst for bettering the lives of Korean women. Reality was much more complicated.
item.dc:creator: Cho Nam-Joo

media:content.media:description: Women in Seoul protesting South Korea’s ban on abortion on April 11, 2019. The law was ruled unconstitutional the same day. The banner reads, “We are not a tool for giving birth.”

item.title: Double Strawberry Shortcakes and Berry Sugar Cookie Bars for July 4
item.description: Sweet treats for the stars and stripes.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Mark Weinberg for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Yossy Arefi
media:content.media:description: Yossy Arefi’s sugar cookie bars with berries.

item.title: Shipping remains far below prewar levels in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.description: A preliminary U.S.-Iran deal took steps to reopen the vital waterway, but traffic remains erratic.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe

media:content.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: After Andy Burnham’s Win, the UK Is Set to Test How Far Charisma Can Shift Electoral Dynamics
item.description: The governing Labour Party may oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer in favor of the more popular Andy Burnham — who will still face all the same challenges.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham speaking after winning a special election as a member of Parliament for Makerfield, in northwest England, on Friday. He received almost 55 percent of the vote, easily besting the candidate from Reform U.K.

item.title: Trump Was Right. The War Ended With a Surrender.
item.description: Each generation seems destined to learn anew the lesson that when hawks declare that a war will be painless, beware.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kristof
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jason Hendardy for The New York Times

item.title: What Makes JD Vance Tick?
item.description: Is deep-seated anxiety what’s driving JD Vance? The Opinion writer Michelle Cottle argues on “The Opinions” that the vice president’s new book, “Communion,” reveals that he remains trapped by childhood scars and a hypocritical obsession with the elite institutions he publicly denounces.

item.title: ‘A Deeply Anxious Personality’: 3 Opinion Writers on JD Vance’s New Book
item.description: Three Opinion writers share their views on “Communion,” the vice president’s new book.
item.dc:creator: E.J. Dionne Jr., Carlos Lozada, Michelle Cottle and Vishakha Darbha

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photo by Alex Wong/Getty

item.title: World Cup Updates: Smaller U.S. Cities Give Visiting Teams an Extra-Warm Embrace
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Julie Holder for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A poster of the Spanish team by the hotel they are staying at.
